susceptibilities.conditions: Humid conditions, rain during ripening, poor air circulation
susceptibilities.notes: Pinot Noir's tight clusters trap moisture between berries, creating ideal conditions for botrytis. Unlike in white varieties where botrytis can be beneficial (noble rot), grey rot destroys red wine quality by degrading color and producing off-flavors.
management: Leaf removal in fruit zone to improve airflow
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Degraded color, oxidized flavors, loss of fruit character, musty or moldy notes

susceptibilities.conditions: Warm, dry conditions with high humidity; shade and poor air circulation
susceptibilities.notes: Pinot Noir's thin skin provides less natural protection against powdery mildew than thicker-skinned varieties. The fungus appears as white powdery coating on leaves, shoots, and fruit. Infected berries may split, inviting secondary infections.
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Reduced yields, off-flavors described as musty or moldy, impaired fermentation

susceptibilities.conditions: Wet, humid conditions; rain and temperatures above 10°C
susceptibilities.notes: Less problematic for Pinot Noir than powdery mildew in most regions. Appears as oily spots on leaves with white fuzzy growth underneath. Can defoliate vines and damage fruit if untreated.
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Primarily affects vine health and yield rather than direct wine quality

susceptibilities.conditions: Present in most wine regions worldwide; spreads through soil and plant material
susceptibilities.notes: The aphid-like pest that devastated European vineyards in the late 1800s. Pinot Noir, like all Vitis vinifera, is susceptible. Phylloxera feeds on roots, eventually killing the vine. Nearly all Pinot Noir worldwide is now grafted onto resistant American rootstocks.
management: Sandy soils can provide natural protection
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Vine death if ungrafted; rootstock selection affects vine vigor and fruit character

susceptibilities.conditions: Enters through pruning wounds during wet weather
susceptibilities.notes: A trunk disease that slowly kills vine cordons and eventually the entire plant. Symptoms include stunted shoots with small, chlorotic leaves. More common in older vineyards. Pinot Noir is moderately susceptible.
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Reduced yields, eventual vine death, replanting costs

susceptibilities.conditions: Stress conditions; enters through wounds; associated with older vines
susceptibilities.notes: A complex of fungal pathogens causing trunk disease. Symptoms include tiger-striped leaves and berry spotting. Can cause sudden vine collapse (apoplexy) in hot weather. Increasingly problematic worldwide.
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Reduced yields, vine death, inconsistent fruit quality

susceptibilities.conditions: Damaged berries, high humidity, presence of fruit flies
susceptibilities.notes: A complex involving yeasts, bacteria, and fungi that causes berry breakdown with a distinctive vinegar smell. Often follows damage from birds, insects, or botrytis. Pinot Noir's thin skin and tight clusters increase vulnerability.
management: Controlling botrytis and other primary damage
Early harvest if pressure is high
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Volatile acidity, vinegar notes, degraded color, undrinkable wine if severe

susceptibilities.conditions: Spread by dagger nematodes in soil; infected plant material
susceptibilities.notes: Causes fan-shaped leaves, short internodes, and reduced yields. Can reduce vine lifespan significantly. No cure exists; infected vines must be removed. Soil must be fumigated before replanting.
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Reduced yields, uneven ripening, shorter vine life

susceptibilities.conditions: Spread by mealybugs and scale insects; infected plant material
susceptibilities.notes: Causes leaf margins to roll downward and turn red (in red varieties) in autumn. Delays ripening and reduces sugar accumulation. Increasingly problematic in many regions.
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Lower sugar levels, reduced color, delayed ripening, herbaceous flavors

resistances.notes: Pinot Noir shows moderate tolerance compared to some varieties, though still susceptible. Less commonly grown in high-pressure areas like Southern California.

disease_profile.general_notes: Pinot Noir's thin skin, tight clusters, and early budding make it one of the more disease-prone major varieties. This susceptibility is a primary reason yields in Burgundy are lower than in other French wine regions, and why careful viticulture is essential for quality production.
